race_name: The Assault on Mt. Mitchell — Beast of the East

top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 102, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
11,000ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic The Assault on Mt. Mitchell — Beast of the East's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
107ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 102 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
The Assault on Mt. Mitchell — Beast of the East's paved roads from Piedmont through Appalachian forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 102 miles.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of The Assault on Mt. Mitchell — Beast of the East. Train it until it feels boring.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 11,000ft of climbing on paved roads from Piedmont through Appalachian does to your power file on race day.
102 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
At The Assault on Mt. Mitchell — Beast of the East, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 102 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Tempo is The Assault on Mt. Mitchell — Beast of the East's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 102 miles on paved roads from Piedmont through Appalachian. Train it until it's automatic.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for The Assault on Mt. Mitchell — Beast of the East’s 102 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at The Assault on Mt. Mitchell — Beast of the East. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, VO2max, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 102 miles of paved roads from piedmont through appalachian foothills to alpine summit in Spartanburg, South Carolina to Mt. Mitchell, North Carolina.
